file.content:
# Handoff: Fault A follow-ups (F1 sticky error row + F2 unsurfaced abnormal end)

Run this in a fresh session (recommended: `/yolo-ship` for the parts that are in
scope; see the scope call below). It is self-contained — read the linked memory
notes for deeper context but you don't strictly need this session's history.

## Background

PR **#137** (MERGED) fixed the chat-qa-sweep ship-blocker **Fault A**: when the
serving runner pod dies mid-turn, the host now fires a `chat:turn-error
{reqId, reason}` broadcast → channel-web's per-connection SSE subscriber emits a
`{reqId, error}` frame + closes → the client transport emits an AI-SDK `error`
chunk → `useChat({onError})` → `applyTurnError` flips the `AgentStatus` row to
**error + retry** (`regenerate()`). Memory: `project_fault_a_sse_turn_error_pr137`,
`project_chat_qa_sweep_2026_05_23`. Design rationale (esp. the reqId-vs-
conversationId matching choice): `.claude/memory/decisions.md` entries dated
2026-05-23 ("Fault A …").

The **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk** (2026-05-24, on `kind-ax-next-dev`, image rebuilt
from merged main) verified the CORE fix PASSES — kill the serving pod mid-turn →
red "The agent stopped unexpectedly. Retry to continue." + retry button within
~1s; host log `pod_exited code:143` → `chat_turn_error reason:sandbox-exit-before-
chat-end` → `pod_killed`. But it surfaced two follow-ups below.

The Fault A walk scenario is documented in `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md`
("Scenario: sandbox death mid-turn → error+retry (Fault A)").

---

## F1 — Sticky error row (client regression from #137). IN SCOPE — fix it.

**Symptom:** Once `agentStatusActions` enters error mode, it never resets on a
later turn. After ANY turn-error, the status row above the composer stays stuck
(stale text + retry button, error styling) through the retry turn AND every
subsequent turn / new session — until a full page reload (reload clears it; it's
in-memory module state). The assistant's replies still render correctly in the
timeline; only the status row is wrong. `agentStatusActions.error` was dead code
in prod before #137, so this only surfaced once #137 wired `onError` to it.

**Root cause:** `packages/channel-web/src/components/AgentStatus.tsx` →
`RunningEffect`. On mount (turn running) it only `show('Thinking…')`s when
`mode === 'hidden'`; on unmount it only `hide()`s when `mode === 'working'`.
There is no `error → working` transition, so a stale error mode never clears when
a new turn starts.

**Fix:** when a new turn starts (RunningEffect mount / `running` becomes true),
reset a stale `'error'` to working (`show('Thinking…')`) — i.e. broaden the mount
gate from `mode === 'hidden'` to "not already `working`". KEEP the unmount
behavior (hide only when `'working'`) so that an error set *during* a turn still
persists after the turn ends — that persistence is the whole point of #137.
Net behavior: error set mid-turn → persists after the turn (good); next turn
starts → error clears to "Thinking…" (good); that turn succeeds → hides (good);
that turn errors → onError re-sets error (good).

**Test:** `packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/agent-status.test.tsx`. Add cases:
(a) error mode + RunningEffect mounts → resets to working; (b) working + unmount
→ hides; (c) error set while running, then unmount → error PERSISTS (not hidden).
RED-before per CLAUDE.md Bug Fix Policy.

---

## F2 — Abnormal turn-end not surfaced (runner-reported / early-spawn death).

During the walk, clicking **retry** spawned a fresh runner that **crashed on its
own** (`pod_exited` code:1, ~2s after `pod_ready`) and the client got stuck on
"Starting sandbox…" with **no** `chat_turn_error` logged. Two distinct issues:

### F2a — Runner crashes (code 1) replaying an interrupted transcript. INVESTIGATE FIRST.

The retry re-runs the last user turn against a fresh sandbox; the conversation's
last assistant turn was interrupted mid-stream by the kill. The SDK runner appears
to crash (exit 1) when resuming/replaying that interrupted transcript. This is
**runner-side** (`packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/`), adjacent to the
runner-owned-sessions / transcript work (PRs #125, #127, #132). It may be the more
important bug — F2b below only matters because the runner crashes here.

- **Capture the crash:** the runner pod is deleted fast. Repro on kind (kill pod
  mid-turn, then retry) and grab `kubectl -n ax-next-runners logs <pod> --previous`
  immediately, or add a brief sleep before teardown, or reproduce the
  resume-from-interrupted-transcript locally in `agent-claude-sdk-runner` tests.
- Runner code is baked into the image → **image-rebuild loop** required to verify
  (per `docker-build-cache-runner-fixes`); the fast hostPath loop won't cover it.
- Decide root cause (malformed/partial jsonl on resume? SDK `query({resume})`
  choking on a truncated last turn?) and fix + a runner regression test.

### F2b — Host doesn't surface a runner-reported / early-spawn terminated end. IN SCOPE.

#137 fires `chat:turn-error` only when the ORCHESTRATOR synthesizes the terminated
outcome (the `if (!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` chokepoints + the
`session:terminate` subscriber). It does NOT fire when:

1. **The runner reports a terminated outcome via `event.chat-end`** (no turn-end).
   The IPC server fires `chat:end` → `onChatEnd` resolves the deferred →
   `resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` stays `true` → the `fireTurnError` chokepoint is
   skipped, and since no `chat:turn-end` fired, the SSE never gets a terminal frame
   → hangs. (This is the most likely cause of the walk's stuck "Starting sandbox…":
   the crashing runner POSTed chat-end-with-error before dying.)
2. **Early-spawn terminated returns** — `queue-work-failed`, `sandbox-open-failed`
   (and arguably `chat:start:*`, `agent-resolve:*`) — return early firing `chat:end`
   but were deliberately NOT wired to `fireTurnError` in #137.

**Fix — surface a turn-error on ANY terminated end:**
- `packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts`:
  - In `onChatEnd(ctx, payload)`: if `payload.outcome.kind !== 'complete'`, fire
    `chat:turn-error`. **CRITICAL matching gotcha:** the IPC-fired `chat:end`
    RESTAMPS `ctx.reqId` (fresh per request), so firing with `ctx.reqId` will NOT
    match the SSE connection (which matches the ORIGINAL reqId). This path must be
    matched by **conversationId** (like the existing done-frame `chat:turn-end`
    subscriber in sse.ts), not reqId. Carry/echo conversationId so the SSE can
    match. Re-read the decisions.md entry on why #137's turn-error matches by reqId
    (orchestrator-fired, original reqId) before changing this.
  - Wire `fireTurnError` into the early-spawn terminated returns (`queue-work-failed`,
    `sandbox-open-failed`) — those still hold the original `ctx.reqId`, so reqId
    matching works there.
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/sse.ts`: extend the per-connection
  `chat:turn-error` subscriber to ALSO match by `ctx.conversationId === conversationId`
  (not only `payload.reqId === reqId`) — mirroring the done-frame subscriber — so a
  runner-reported terminated end (restamped reqId) still emits the error frame.
- Watch the idempotency: a turn that already got a `done` frame (closed SSE) then a
  late terminated `chat:end` must be a harmless no-op (SSE already closed). And a
  `complete` outcome must NEVER fire turn-error.

**Tests:**
- orchestrator: `onChatEnd` with a terminated outcome fires `chat:turn-error`
  (matched by conversationId); `queue-work-failed` / `sandbox-open-failed` fire it
  (by reqId); a `complete` outcome does NOT.
- sse: a `chat:turn-error` matched by conversationId (no reqId match) emits the
  error frame + closes; a `complete`/normal turn-end path is unaffected.

---

## Scope call

- **F1** and **F2b** are squarely the Fault A error-surfacing feature's
  completion — do them (one PR is fine; they're cohesive).
- **F2a** is a separate runner-side bug. Recommend investigating it first (it's
  likely the higher-impact fix, and F2b's stuck-state only manifests because the
  runner crashes). Decide whether to fold F2a into the same PR or split — but do
  NOT silently drop it (TODO/memory). If F2a is fixed, F2b is the safety net for
  any other runner-reported error.
- Faults **B/D** (host bounce / network drop → silent finalize) remain a separate
  pre-existing TODO — out of scope here.

## Verification (kind `ax-next-dev`)

Cluster is currently running the #137 fix image. Walk method:
- Port-forward: `kubectl -n ax-next port-forward svc/ax-next-host 9090:9090`.
- Auth: mint `ax_auth_session` from a live `auth_better_v1_sessions` token
  (DB **`ax_next`** on the postgres pod) signed via `parseCookieKey` +
  `signCookieValue` from `packages/http-server/dist/cookies.js`, key =
  `ax-next-secrets/http-cookie-key`; plant in the browser with Playwright
  `document.cookie='ax_auth_session=<wire>; Path=/'` (HttpOnly isn't enforced on JS
  writes). See `reference_headless_authed_chat_kind`.
- F1 check: any turn-error → error row → start a new turn → assert the row resets
  to "Thinking…" then hides (no stale stuck row); reload not required.
- F2 check: kill the serving pod mid-turn → click retry → assert the retry either
  completes (F2a fixed) OR surfaces error+retry again (F2b) — never stuck on
  "Starting sandbox…".
- F2a (runner) needs an **image rebuild** to verify (`docker-build-cache-runner-fixes`).
  F1 + F2b are host-side TS → fast hostPath dist loop OR rebuild.

Gate before PR: `pnpm build` + `pnpm -r run test` + eslint on changed files
(scope eslint to changed files — repo-wide lint trips on stale worktree dirs).

Fixing needs live cluster repro + an image-rebuild loop ([[feedback_docker_build_cache_runner_fixes]]) + a different package (agent-claude-sdk-runner). F2b is the safety net that surfaces the user-visible stuck-"Starting sandbox…" regardless. A speculative jsonl-repair fix without a repro violates systematic-debugging + Bug Fix Policy (no meaningful regression test without the failure mode). | Fold F2a in (rejected: different package + verify loop, speculative fix, blows scope+context); silently drop (rejected: handoff forbids) |
61:| 2026-05-24 | F2b core insight: `onChatEnd` is a **`chat:end` subscriber** (plugin.ts:105), so EVERY `bus.fire('chat:end')` — runner-reported IPC + orchestrator early-returns + chokepoints — re-enters it. Surface the turn-error IN `onChatEnd`, guarded by a new `wasInFlight` return from `resolveWaiterFor` (true iff it resolved a previously-UNSETTLED waiter) AND `outcome.kind !== 'complete'` | `wasInFlight` cleanly separates three cases without double/spurious fires: (a) runner-reported live crash → waiter unsettled → fire (matched by conversationId, reqId restamped); (b) chokepoint paths → deferred already settled before their `bus.fire('chat:end')` → onChatEnd skips, their OWN explicit fireTurnError (kept from #137) is the single fire; (c) reaped-warm-runner late terminated chat:end after a completed turn → waiter gone → no spurious log/event. | Centralize ALL turn-error firing in onChatEnd + remove the chokepoint fires (rejected: spurious `chat_turn_error` logs when a reaped warm runner POSTs a late terminated chat:end after a success); fire unconditionally on kind!=='complete' (rejected: double-fires at chokepoints + spurious at reaped runners) |
62:| 2026-05-24 | onChatEnd-fired turn-error matched at the SSE by `ctx.conversationId === conversationId` (OR the existing reqId match); early-spawn returns (3 sites: routed+fresh `queue-work-failed`, fresh `sandbox-open-failed`) get explicit `fireTurnError` matched by **reqId** | The IPC listener restamps `ctx.reqId` per request but stamps `ctx.conversationId` (listener.ts:144) — so the runner-reported path can only join the SSE by conversationId (mirrors the existing done-frame `chat:turn-end` subscriber, sse.ts:271). The early-returns `unregisterWaiter` BEFORE firing chat:end → onChatEnd's wasInFlight=false skips them → they need their own explicit fire, and still hold the original `ctx.reqId` so reqId-match works. `initCtx` in sse.test has no conversationId so the existing reqId-mismatch negative test stays green. | Match the onChatEnd path by reqId (rejected: restamped, never matches); rely on onChatEnd for early-returns too (rejected: waiter unregistered first → wasInFlight=false → wouldn't fire) |

64:| 2026-05-24 | **CodeRabbit fix (supersedes the conversationId-match half of decision 63):** `resolveWaiterFor` returns the resolved ORIGINAL reqId (`string \| undefined`); onChatEnd fires turn-error with it; **SSE reverted to reqId-only matching, conversationId-match removed entirely** | CodeRabbit (Major, correct): the boolean discarded the original reqId, so onChatEnd fired with the IPC-restamped ctx.reqId → SSE fell back to conversationId, which is too coarse — two concurrent invokes can share a conversation/sessionId (orchestrator.ts:458-473), so it could close a co-resident turn's stream. `resolveWaiterFor` ALREADY computes the original reqId (firstReqId in the sessionId fallback) — returning it lets onChatEnd fire the precise per-turn key, so the SSE matches by reqId (its #137 behavior) and the coarse fallback is gone by construction. Verified the concurrent-invoke scenario is real before adopting (receiving-code-review). | Keep conversationId-match as belt-and-suspenders (rejected: retains the exact coarse-match risk CodeRabbit flagged); push back (rejected: concern is valid + the file documents shared-sessionId concurrent invokes) |

The prior session (decision 60) already established F2a's shape: the runner catches the resume exception and POSTs `chat-end{terminated}` before `exit 1`, so the crash is inside the SDK's `query({resume})` reading a truncated jsonl — not statically determinable, needs a repro. 547	        parent_tool_use_id: null,
548	        // Cast: SDKUserMessage.message.content is typed `string` today, but

559	  let terminatedError:
560	    | { name: string; message: string; stack?: string }
561	    | undefined;
562	
563	  try {
564	    const queryIter = query({
565	      prompt: userMessages(),
566	      options: {
567	        // Phase C: SDK resume(sessionId). When the conversation has a
568	        // bound runner session id, the SDK rehydrates the transcript
569	        // from its own on-disk store under HOME (workspaceRoot, see
570	        // below). Spread-conditional so the field is OMITTED on first
571	        // boot — the SDK's `resume?: string` typing is "string or
572	        // missing", not "string or null"; passing `undefined` would be
573	        // a type-level rather than a wire-level signal.
574	        ...(runnerSessionId !== null ? { resume: runnerSessionId } : {}),
575	        // ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is the `ax-cred:<hex>` placeholder (substituted
576	        // by the credential-proxy mid-flight); no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL — SDK
577	        // calls api.anthropic.com directly through HTTPS_PROXY.
578	        //
579	        // The env literal here partitions into two distinct concerns that
580	        // happen to share the same SDK subprocess env namespace:
581	        //
582	        // (a) Phase C — HOME redirect for the SDK subprocess (jsonl
583	        //     persistence). See per-bullet rationale below.
584	        //
585	        // (b) Phase 0 skill discovery (I-P0-1 / I-P0-3) — CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
586	        //     forwarded from the sandbox-provided runner env so the SDK's
587	        //     `'user'` setting source resolves to a host-owned root
588	        //     (`<sandbox-HOME>/.ax/session`) that's SEPARATE from the
589	        //     workspace's `'project'` source (`<cwd>/.claude/skills`).
590	        //     Without the forward, the SDK falls back to `<HOME>/.claude`,
591	        //     which — because the (a) override below sets HOME=workspaceRoot
592	        //     — collapses onto the project-source path, making the two
593	        //     setting sources indistinguishable and rendering the host-
594	        //     installed-skills surface unreachable. The forward itself
595	        //     lives in proxy-startup.ts (ENV_ALLOWLIST) so the value
596	        //     arrives via `...proxyStartup.anthropicEnv` below.
597	        //
598	        // (a)/(b) interact via the SDK's per-session jsonl path. The
599	        // SDK derives `$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/projects/<encoded-cwd>/
600	        // <sid>.jsonl` from the same var that drives skill discovery,
601	        // so once (b) moved CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR outside the workspace,
602	        // the SDK's turn-transcript writes went with it — and the
603	        // turn-end `git add -A` stopped capturing them. The fix lives
604	        // upstream of this env literal: scaffoldSdkProjectsSymlink (in
605	        // git-workspace.ts, called from the materialize block above)
606	        // creates `$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/projects` as a symlink into
607	        // `<workspaceRoot>/.claude/projects`, so the writes land inside
608	        // `/permanent` and the bundler picks them up. The (b) split
609	        // and the jsonl capture are restored independently; no env
610	        // change here.
611	        //
612	        // Phase C: HOME redirect for the SDK subprocess.
613	        //   - The k8s sandbox pod sets HOME=/nonexistent at the pod level
614	        //     so `git` (and any other tool the runner spawns) can't
615	        //     accidentally read a global ~/.gitconfig — git-paranoia.
616	        //   - The SDK needs HOME pointed at the workspace so its native
617	        //     ~/.claude/projects/<sessionId>.jsonl lands where the
618	        //     turn-end `git status + git add -A + bundle` captures it,
619	        //     closing the jsonl gap that workspace Phase 3 set up the
620	        //     plumbing for.
621	        //   - The runner-process git operations inherit HOME=/nonexistent
622	        //     from process.env (we don't override their env), so the
623	        //     redirect is targeted to this SDK subprocess only.
624	        //   - Side effect: the SDK's auxiliary files (`.claude.json`,
625	        //     `.claude/backups/`, etc.) also land in the workspace.
626	        //     Acceptable trade-off (Q1 of the Phase C plan): the `.ax/`
627	        //     filter in workspace:pre-apply doesn't subscribe validators
628	        //     to them, and we can split with a symlink/copy step in a
629	        //     follow-up if needed.
630	        //   - HOME is spread AFTER ...proxyStartup.anthropicEnv so this
631	        //     value wins on conflict. anthropicEnv currently doesn't set
632	        //     HOME, but defensive ordering matches the intent: we
633	        //     explicitly redirect HOME for the SDK subprocess.
634	        //   - We DO NOT override CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR here — the sandbox
635	        //     plugin's value (carried through proxyStartup.anthropicEnv)
636	        //     is the source of truth for the (b) split above. If a future
637	        //     refactor adds CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR after the spread it would
638	        //     break I-P0-1.
639	        env: {
640	          ...proxyStartup.anthropicEnv,
641	          HOME: env.workspaceRoot,
642	          // Redirect npx/uvx fetch caches onto the ephemeral tier so they
643	          // don't land in HOME=/permanent and get bundled to the host each
644	          // turn. No-op ({}) when no ephemeral root was wired. See
645	          // tool-cache-env.ts. Spread AFTER HOME so an ephemeral root always
646	          // wins for the cache vars (HOME stays the workspace root).
647	          ...buildToolCacheEnv(env.ephemeralRoot),
648	          // Activate the session Python venv (PATH + VIRTUAL_ENV + pip CA
649	          // trust) so `pip install` reaches the venv and trusts the proxy
650	          // MITM CA. Gated on the scaffold actually succeeding (pythonVenvReady).
651	          // Spread AFTER anthropicEnv so PATH/VIRTUAL_ENV win. caCertFile is the
652	          // same proxy CA PEM the Node/uv tools already trust (SSL_CERT_FILE /
653	          // NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, forwarded by proxy-startup). See python-venv.ts.
654	          ...buildPythonVenvEnv({
655	            ephemeralRoot: pythonVenvReady ? env.ephemeralRoot : undefined,
656	            currentPath: proxyStartup.anthropicEnv.PATH,
657	            caCertFile:
658	              proxyStartup.anthropicEnv.SSL_CERT_FILE ??
659	              proxyStartup.anthropicEnv.NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS,
660	          }),
661	        },
662	        cwd: env.workspaceRoot,
663	        // Session-scoped scratch tier. When the sandbox provided an
664	        // ephemeral root (k8s: the `/ephemeral` emptyDir mount; subprocess:
665	        // a per-session tempdir), grant the SDK's file tools access to it
666	        // BEYOND cwd. Without this the file tools are bounded to cwd
667	        // (`/permanent`), so any "temporary" file the agent writes lands in
668	        // the workspace tree and gets `git add -A`'d + bundled to the host
669	        // at turn end. additionalDirectories lets the agent stage throwaway

content.text:
**Root cause reproduced deterministically.** When resuming a sessionId whose transcript has no parseable conversation messages — jsonl **missing (C), empty (D), metadata-only (E), user-line truncated mid-write (F), or garbage (G)** — the SDK throws:

```
Error: Claude Code returned an error result: No conversation found with session ID: <sessionId>
```

The runner catches this → `exit 1` → `chat-end{terminated}`. This matches the cluster symptom exactly ("~2s after pod_ready", before any real turn). The architecture explains it: commits happen **only at turn-end**, so a turn killed before its first commit leaves the host with a **bound sessionId but no resumable transcript**. On retry, `query({resume: sessionId})` → "No conversation found" → crash. The "truncated/dangling-tool_use" hypothesis was wrong; the SDK is robust to those.

Now I need the sessionId binding lifecycle to design the correct fix. Let me trace it:

140	  //
141	  // The bearer token in env.authToken is the SAME token the host used to
142	  // mint this session — the IPC server resolves it to ctx.sessionId, and
143	  // the session backend reads its own row keyed by that. There's no
144	  // sessionId on the wire; the runner cannot ask for someone else's
145	  // config.
146	  let agentConfig: SessionGetConfigResponse['agentConfig'];
147	  let conversationId: string | null = null;
148	  let runnerSessionId: string | null = null;
149	  try {
150	    const cfg = (await client.call(
151	      'session.get-config',
152	      {},
153	    )) as SessionGetConfigResponse;
154	    agentConfig = cfg.agentConfig;
155	    // Task 15 (Week 10–12): the host populates conversationId at session-
156	    // creation time when the runner is for an existing conversation. The
157	    // runner uses a non-null value as the trigger to bind the SDK
158	    // session id back via `conversation.store-runner-session` after
159	    // first init. We normalize `undefined` (older host that hasn't

161	    // same skip-bind branch via a strict equality check on the string
162	    // type.
163	    conversationId = typeof cfg.conversationId === 'string' ? cfg.conversationId : null;
164	    // Phase E (2026-05-09): runnerSessionId rides the same response now
165	    // that `conversation.fetch-history` is gone. Non-null = the SDK has
166	    // bound a session id on a prior boot; we pass it as `options.resume`
167	    // to `query()` below so the SDK rehydrates from its own on-disk
168	    // transcript instead of starting a fresh conversation. Null = first
169	    // boot OR conversationId is null; the SDK starts fresh.
170	    //
171	    // Empty string is treated as null. The wire schema is
172	    // `z.string().nullable()` (no `.min(1)`), so a future bug or stale
173	    // row could in principle deliver `''`. Passing `resume: ''` to the
174	    // SDK is undefined behavior; coerce defensively.
175	    runnerSessionId =
176	      typeof cfg.runnerSessionId === 'string' && cfg.runnerSessionId.length > 0
177	        ? cfg.runnerSessionId

content.text:
I now have the confirmed root cause and full binding lifecycle:

- **Bind happens at `system/init`** (main.ts:774) — within ~1s of turn start — via `conversation.store-runner-session`.
- **Transcript commits only at turn-end** (main.ts:995/1149).
- **The gap:** a turn killed after init-bind but before turn-end commit leaves the host with `runner_session_id = X` but **no resumable transcript for X**. On retry, `query({resume: X})` → "No conversation found" → `exit 1`.

The fix is a **runner-side resume guard**: verify a resumable transcript exists before passing `resume`; if not, omit it and start fresh. The critical risk is whether the host **accepts a rebind** to the fresh session_id. Let me check that handler, and run one more repro to pin the exact guard predicate (does a lone user line resume?).

content.text:
Findings: SDK has **no "start fresh with a given sessionId"** option (only `resume` needs an existing session; `forkSession` creates a *new* id). And it exposes `deleteSession()`. Repro H/I confirm: resume succeeds with ≥1 parseable user/assistant message; fails only when there are none. 

So a runner-only "omit resume" fix would mint a fresh id Y, then the init-bind hits `conflict` (host still bound to X) → another `exit 1`. The fix needs to clear the stale binding. Let me check whether an unbind hook is exposed to the runner and how it's registered:

content.text:
`unbindSession` clears `active_session_id`, **not** `runner_session_id` — so there's no existing primitive to clear a stale binding, and the host bind is genuinely once-only.

This reframes the fix. The real defect is that **the bind fires at `system/init` (premature) but the transcript only becomes durable at turn-end**. A turn killed in between persists a binding that points at nothing. The clean root fix: **bind `runner_session_id` at the first durable turn-end commit, not at init** — then the premature stale binding never forms, retry sees `NULL` → clean fresh start. No host change, no rebind/conflict.

Let me read the turn-end commit + turn-end-event path precisely to place the bind and check the `runnerSessionId` vs `transcriptSessionId` usage:

# F2a — runner exits 1 resuming a session with no committed transcript

## Problem (repro-confirmed, 2026-05-24)

When a serving runner pod is killed mid-turn, the retry's fresh runner crashes
`exit 1` ~2s after `pod_ready`. The TODO/handoff hypothesized a *truncated /
dangling-tool_use* transcript. A **deterministic local real-SDK repro**
(`@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119` darwin binary + a real API key)
**disproved** that: the SDK resume is robust to a truncated final line AND a
dangling tool_use (both succeed).

The real failure is:

```
Error: Claude Code returned an error result: No conversation found with session ID: <X>
```

`query({ resume: X })` throws this whenever the jsonl for `X` has **no parseable
user/assistant message** (missing / empty / metadata-only / user-line truncated
mid-write / garbage). The SDK catch in `main.ts:1170` → `exit 1` →
`event.chat-end{terminated}`.

### Why a bound session can point at nothing

- The runner commits the transcript to the host **only at turn-end**
  (`main.ts:995` per-turn, `:1149` final). A turn killed before its first
  commit never persists a transcript.
- BUT the host **bind** (`conversation.store-runner-session`) fires at
  **`system/init`** (`main.ts:774`), ~1s into the turn — *before* durability.
- Kill in that window → host conversation row has `runner_session_id = X` but
  the workspace git has no resumable transcript for `X`.
- Retry → fresh pod → `get-config` returns `runner_session_id = X` →
  `query({ resume: X })` → "No conversation found" → `exit 1`.

The host bind is **once-only** (`conversations/plugin.ts:1003` → `conflict` on a
different value), and the SDK has **no "start fresh with a given id"** option, so
simply omitting `resume` and minting a new id would hit `conflict` → another
`exit 1`.

## Fix

**Root fix — defer the bind to the first durable commit (purely runner-side):**
Move `conversation.store-runner-session` from the `system/init` handler to fire
**once, after the first host-ACCEPTED turn-end commit** (`commitNotifyWithResync`
→ `outcome === 'accepted'`). Gate: `conversationId !== null` AND we started
without an incoming binding (`runnerSessionId === null` at boot) AND not yet
bound. Now `runner_session_id` is set **iff** a resumable transcript is durable
on the host — the stale-binding window cannot form, retry sees `NULL` → clean
fresh start. No host change; once-only bind invariant intact. Bind failure is
self-healing (leave the flag unset → next accepted commit / final commit
retries) rather than fail-loud, since the turn already streamed to the user.

**Defense-in-depth — resume guard:** add `hasResumableTranscript(workspaceRoot,
sessionId)`; before `query`, if a bound session has no parseable transcript,
**omit `resume` + log** instead of letting the SDK hard-crash. With the root fix
this should never fire, but it converts any future bound-but-missing-transcript
(a materialize/git regression) from a hard `exit 1` into graceful degradation.

## Tasks (independent, testable; INLINE strict TDD)

- **T1 — `hasResumableTranscript` helper + unit tests** (`turn-end-uuid.ts`,
  `turn-end-uuid.test.ts`). Predicate: locate the jsonl (reuse `locateJsonl`),
  return true iff ≥1 line JSON-parses with `type ∈ {user, assistant}`. RED→GREEN
  against the repro matrix: missing/empty/metadata-only/truncated-user/garbage →
  false; lone-user / user+assistant → true.

- **T2 — resume guard in `main.ts`** + `main.test.ts`: when `runnerSessionId` is
  set but `!hasResumableTranscript`, omit `resume` (compute `resumeSessionId`).
  Test: stale binding → query called WITHOUT `resume`; valid binding → WITH.

- **T3 — move the bind to first-accepted-commit** (`main.ts` init handler +
  `bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded` called after each accepted commit) + `main.test.ts`:
  (a) no `store-runner-session` at init; (b) bound after the first accepted
  per-turn commit (fresh boot, `conversationId` set); (c) NOT bound when we
  resumed (boot `runnerSessionId` non-null); (d) a turn that never commits
  (killed) leaves it unbound — the regression test that "would have caught" F2a.

## Out of scope / follow-ups (→ TODO.md)

- **Cluster MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk** (image-rebuild loop): kill serving pod
  during the conversation's FIRST turn → retry → assert it completes (not stuck /
  not crash-looping). Runner code is baked into the image.
- Pre-existing: turn-end `turnId` reads use the boot `runnerSessionId`
  (`main.ts:1086/1113`), so a fresh first turn emits no `turnId` (should use
  `transcriptSessionId`). Unrelated to the crash; note only.

## Boundary review

No hook-surface change (`conversations:store-runner-session` signature
unchanged — only its call SITE moved). No new IPC action, capability, dependency,
or untrusted-content ingestion (the guard reads only line `.type`). No
boundary-review / security-checklist trigger fires.

+  // F2a resume guard (defense-in-depth). `query({ resume: X })` hard-crashes
+  // the runner (`exit 1` → chat-end `terminated`) with "No conversation found
+  // with session ID: X" whenever the bound session has NO parseable transcript
+  // in the materialized workspace. The host bind is now deferred to the first
+  // durable commit (see bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded), so a bound id should always
+  // have a transcript — but if a materialize/git regression ever drops it, omit
+  // `resume` and start fresh instead of crashing. Verified against the live SDK
+  // is exactly what triggers the crash; a real user/assistant transcript
+  // resumes cleanly even with a truncated trailing line.
+      'runner: bound runner session has no resumable transcript in the workspace; starting fresh instead of resuming\n',
+  // F2a root fix: bind the conversation row → runner-native transcript ONCE,
+  // after the first host-ACCEPTED turn-end commit — NOT at `system/init`.
+  // Binding at init persisted `runner_session_id` ~1s into the turn, BEFORE the
+  // transcript is durable on the host (commits fire only at turn-end). A turn
+  // killed in that window left a binding that points at nothing, so the retry's
+  // `query({ resume })` crashed with "No conversation found". Deferring the bind
+  // to a host-accepted commit makes `runner_session_id` set IFF a resumable
+  // transcript exists → a killed-before-commit turn leaves it NULL → the retry
+  // starts fresh cleanly. Gated to the fresh-boot case (`runnerSessionId` null);
+  // a resumed session is already bound on the host. Bind failure is
+  // self-healing — leave the flag unset so the next accepted commit (or the
+  // final commit) retries; the turn already streamed to the user, so failing
+  // the run now would be incoherent.
+        `runner: conversation.store-runner-session failed (will retry on next commit): ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}\n`,

content.text: All 259 pass. But I spotted a correctness gap in the guard's defensive path: `transcriptSessionId` is seeded to the (stale) `runnerSessionId` at boot, so when the guard strips `resume`, the fresh SDK session mints a new id but `transcriptSessionId` stays stale → the flush-wait would watch the wrong jsonl. I must reset it so the init handler re-captures the fresh id:

content.text:
The fix is implemented and the runner package + lint + build are green. I'm now waiting on the full-repo test suite (running in the background across all packages, including the Postgres-backed ones) to confirm no cross-package breakage before the Codex review.

Implementation summary so far:
- **`turn-end-uuid.ts`**: added `hasResumableTranscript()` + 7 unit tests (RED→GREEN).
- **`main.ts`**: (1) resume guard demotes `resume` to a fresh start when the bound session has no resumable transcript; (2) moved the `store-runner-session` bind from `system/init` to the first host-accepted turn-end commit (`bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded`), gated to fresh boot, self-healing on failure.
- **`main.test.ts`**: added the guard test + the F2a regression test (killed-before-commit → no stale bind), and updated the prior init-bind tests to the new commit-backed semantics (incl. bind-failure now non-fatal/self-healing).

I'll continue once the suite reports.

input.command:
cd /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(F2a): defer runner_session_id bind to first durable commit + resume guard

The retry of a turn that was killed mid-stream crashed the fresh runner
`exit 1` ~2s after pod_ready. A live SDK repro (claude-agent-sdk 0.2.119,
2026-05-24) disproved the "truncated/dangling-tool_use transcript" hypothesis
— the SDK resume is robust to both. The real crash is:

  Error: Claude Code returned an error result:
         No conversation found with session ID: <X>

thrown by `query({ resume: X })` whenever the materialized jsonl for X has NO
parseable user/assistant message.

Root cause: commits fire only at turn-end, but the host bind
(`conversation.store-runner-session`) fired at `system/init` (~1s in), BEFORE
the transcript is durable. A turn killed in that window persisted a
`runner_session_id` pointing at nothing, so the retry's resume crashed. The
host bind is once-only, and the SDK has no "start fresh with a given id", so
omitting resume + minting a new id would only hit a bind `conflict`.

Fix:
- Root: defer the bind to the first host-ACCEPTED turn-end commit
  (`bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded`), gated to the fresh-boot case. Now
  `runner_session_id` is set IFF a resumable transcript is durable → a
  killed-before-commit turn leaves it NULL → the retry starts fresh cleanly.
  No host change; the once-only bind invariant is intact. Bind failure is
  self-healing (retried on the next accepted/final commit) rather than
  fail-loud, since the turn already streamed to the user.
- Defense-in-depth: `hasResumableTranscript()` resume guard — if a bound
  session has no resumable transcript in the materialized workspace, omit
  `resume` and start fresh instead of letting the SDK hard-crash.

Tests (Bug Fix Policy):
- turn-end-uuid: hasResumableTranscript matrix (missing/empty/metadata-only/
  truncated-user/garbage → false; lone-user / user+assistant → true).
- main: the bind fires only AFTER an accepted commit; a turn with no committed
  transcript leaves the row UNBOUND (the F2a regression test); the resume
  guard strips `resume` on a stale binding; bind-failure is non-fatal +
  self-heals.

Boundary review: no hook-surface/IPC/capability/dependency change — only the
SITE of an existing `conversations:store-runner-session` call moved. No
security-checklist trigger (guard reads only line `.type`, not content).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "=== committed ==="; git log --oneline -1

87:## Fault A follow-ups (F1 sticky error row + F2b unsurfaced terminated end — PR for `worktree-fault-a-followups-f1-f2b`)
91:- [ ] **F2a — runner exits 1 replaying an interrupted transcript on retry (runner-side, NOT in this PR).** When the serving pod is killed mid-turn, the conversation's last assistant turn is left truncated in the runner-owned jsonl. On retry, a fresh runner resumes via `query({ resume: sessionId })` (`agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:564-574`, `HOME`→workspace at :641) and crashes ~2s after pod_ready. **Investigation (static read, 2026-05-24):** the runner does NO validation/repair of the jsonl before resume — it hands the raw (possibly truncated) transcript to the SDK, which reads `$HOME/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` itself. The runner ALREADY catches the exception and POSTs `event.chat-end{kind:'terminated'}` before `exit 1` (`main.ts:1191-1201`), so the user-visible stuck-"Starting sandbox…" is the *host* not surfacing that terminated end — **fixed by F2b in this PR** (onChatEnd now fires `chat:turn-error` on a runner-reported terminated outcome). The runner crash root cause is INSIDE the SDK's resume of a truncated transcript and is NOT determinable from static reading. **To fix F2a:** repro on `kind-ax-next-dev` (kill serving pod mid-turn → retry; grab `kubectl -n ax-next-runners logs <pod> --previous` immediately, or reproduce resume-from-interrupted-transcript in `agent-claude-sdk-runner` tests), decide the failure mode (truncated final jsonl line? dangling tool_use w/o tool_result?), then fix + a runner regression test. Runner code is baked into the image → **image-rebuild loop required** (per [[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]); the fast hostPath loop won't cover it. F2b is the safety net for any other runner-reported abnormal end regardless.
92:- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (F1 + F2b) on kind.** Host-side TS → fast hostPath dist loop (no image rebuild). F1: any turn-error → red row → start a new turn → assert the row resets to "Thinking…" then hides (no stale stuck row; reload not required). F2b: kill the serving pod mid-turn → click retry → assert the retry either completes (only once F2a is fixed) OR surfaces error+retry again — **never** stuck on "Starting sandbox…". Auth via [[reference_headless_authed_chat_kind]]. Covered by unit tests (orchestrator + sse + agent-status); the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.

On retry, a fresh runner resumes via `query({ resume: sessionId })` (`agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:564-574`, `HOME`→workspace at :641) and crashes ~2s after pod_ready. **Investigation (static read, 2026-05-24):** the runner does NO validation/repair of the jsonl before resume — it hands the raw (possibly truncated) transcript to the SDK, which reads `$HOME/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` itself. The runner ALREADY catches the exception and POSTs `event.chat-end{kind:'terminated'}` before `exit 1` (`main.ts:1191-1201`), so the user-visible stuck-"Starting sandbox…" is the *host* not surfacing that terminated end — **fixed by F2b in this PR** (onChatEnd now fires `chat:turn-error` on a runner-reported terminated outcome). The runner crash root cause is INSIDE the SDK's resume of a truncated transcript and is NOT determinable from static reading. **To fix F2a:** repro on `kind-ax-next-dev` (kill serving pod mid-turn → retry; grab `kubectl -n ax-next-runners logs <pod> --previous` immediately, or reproduce resume-from-interrupted-transcript in `agent-claude-sdk-runner` tests), decide the failure mode (truncated final jsonl line? dangling tool_use w/o tool_result?), then fix + a runner regression test. Runner code is baked into the image → **image-rebuild loop required** (per [[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]); the fast hostPath loop won't cover it. F2b is the safety net for any other runner-reported abnormal end regardless.
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (F1 + F2b) on kind.**
input.new_string:
- [x] ~~**F2a — runner exits 1 replaying an interrupted transcript on retry.**~~ — fixed (PR for `worktree-f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix`). A **live SDK repro** (claude-agent-sdk 0.2.119, 2026-05-24) **disproved** the static "truncated / dangling-tool_use" hypothesis: the SDK resume is robust to both a truncated trailing line AND a dangling tool_use. The real crash is `Error: Claude Code returned an error result: No conversation found with session ID: X`, thrown by `query({ resume: X })` whenever the materialized jsonl for X has **no parseable user/assistant message**. Root cause: commits fire only at turn-end, but the host bind (`conversation.store-runner-session`) fired at `system/init` (~1s in, before durability) — a turn killed in that window persisted a `runner_session_id` pointing at nothing → the retry's resume crashed. Fix = (root) defer the bind to the first host-ACCEPTED turn-end commit (`bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded`, fresh-boot only, self-healing) so `runner_session_id` is set IFF a resumable transcript is durable → killed-before-commit leaves it NULL → retry starts fresh; + (defense-in-depth) a `hasResumableTranscript()` resume guard that omits `resume` instead of letting the SDK hard-crash. No host change; once-only bind invariant intact. Covered by unit tests; cluster walk below.
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (F2a) on kind — image-rebuild loop required.** Runner code is baked into the image ([[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]), so the fast hostPath loop won't cover it. Walk: start a NEW chat → during the FIRST turn kill the serving pod (`kubectl -n ax-next-runners delete pod <pod>`) → click retry → assert the retry **completes** (not stuck on "Starting sandbox…", not a crash-loop). Confirm via `kubectl -n ax-next-runners logs <retry-pod>` that there is NO `No conversation found with session ID` and the conversation row's `runner_session_id` is bound only after the turn commits. Auth via [[reference_headless_authed_chat_kind]]. Covered by unit tests (turn-end-uuid + main); the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.
- [ ] **Pre-existing (noted during F2a, out of scope): turn-end `turnId` uses the boot `runnerSessionId`, not `transcriptSessionId`** (`main.ts` turn-end emissions). On a fresh first turn `runnerSessionId` is null, so no `turnId` is emitted for that turn's `event.turn-end` (drop-turn / routines silence-token can't refer back to it). Switch those reads to `transcriptSessionId` if a real caller needs first-turn turnIds.
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (F1 + F2b) on kind.**

content.content:
**Findings**

- **High - stale resume demotion never repairs the binding.**  
  In [main.ts](/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:577), the guard demotes `resumeSessionId` and clears `transcriptSessionId`, but leaves `runnerSessionId` set to the stale bound id. Then `bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded()` refuses to bind whenever `runnerSessionId !== null` at [main.ts:610](/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:610). Result: the fresh SDK session id captured at [main.ts:831](/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:831) is never stored. The row remains bound to the non-resumable id forever, future boots repeat the demotion, and `conversations:get` will keep reading the stale jsonl path instead of the new transcript. The same stale id is also used for turn-id lookup at [main.ts:1164](/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:1164), so guard-demoted fresh turns can miss or mis-target `turnId`.

- **High - bind errors are swallowed too broadly, including non-retryable conflicts.**  
  `bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded()` catches every `conversation.store-runner-session` failure and logs “will retry” at [main.ts:617](/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:617). That is not safe for 400/404/409 cases. A 409 means another runner/session already bound the conversation to a different value; treating that as transient lets the losing runner keep streaming and committing an orphan transcript. This is the double-bind race the host-side contract is trying to prevent. Persistent bind failure also silently leaves one-turn conversations unbound and unreadable after refresh.

- **Medium - the self-healing test is optimistic compared with production flow.**  
  The new transient-bind-failure test makes `commitTurnAndBundleMock` return a bundle for both per-turn and final commits, so the second bind attempt happens. In production, the per-turn wait is meant to capture the assistant jsonl before the per-turn commit; the final commit at [main.ts:1200](/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:1200) will often be `null`, and no retry happens because binding is only attempted inside `finalBundle !== null` at [main.ts:1205](/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:1205). Add a regression where the first bind fails after an accepted per-turn commit and the final bundle is null.

**Missing Tests**

Add coverage for: guard demotion plus accepted commit repairing or replacing the stale binding; guard demotion turn-id lookup using the fresh session id; persistent bind failure after accepted commit; and 409 conflict from `conversation.store-runner-session` being host-visible or fatal rather than silently retried.

I did not find a new hook-surface or cross-plugin import violation in this diff. The AX invariant concern is state ownership: the stale DB `runner_session_id` and fresh committed transcript can diverge with no repair path.

I attempted the targeted runner tests, but Vitest failed to start in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write `node_modules/.vite-temp/vitest.config...mjs` and hit `EPERM`.

content.text:
Codex returned 3 findings. Let me evaluate each with receiving-code-review discipline.

**Finding 2 (High) is clearly valid:** I conflated transient bind failures (network/5xx → safe to self-heal) with a `conflict` (409 — the conversation is already bound to a *different* id, a real double-bind race). The original code's fatal-on-bind-failure existed precisely to stop a losing runner from committing an orphan; swallowing the conflict defeats that. I must distinguish conflict (terminal) from transient (self-heal).

**Finding 1 (High)** — the demotion-path orphan — is also real, but needs analysis of reachability and the fix's cost. Let me check how the host's `conflict` error reaches the runner (to implement detection) and confirm the demotion-path reasoning:

grep -rn "code\|PluginError\|error\b" packages/ipc-protocol/src/client.ts 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "code|error" | head -20
echo ""
echo "=== client.call error reconstruction ==="; grep -rn "throw\|reject\|new Error\|\.code\|error\." packages/ipc-protocol/src/*.ts | grep -iE "code|error response|reject" | head

1	import { z } from 'zod';
2	
3	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
4	// This file has two halves:
5	//
6	//   1. Wire-error schemas (Zod) — the shared error envelope returned by
7	//      every host IPC handler. Lives below.
8	//
9	//   2. Typed Error classes — thrown by the sandbox-side IPC client when it
10	//      decodes a wire error or hits a transport-level failure. Lives at the
11	//      bottom of this file. The client (also in @ax/ipc-protocol) imports
12	//      them via `./errors.js`.
13	//
14	// Why colocated: callers and decoders share the same vocabulary
15	// (HOST_UNAVAILABLE, SESSION_INVALID, …) and keeping wire-shape + typed-
16	// error in one file makes it harder for the two to drift out of sync.
17	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
18	
19	/**
20	 * The closed set of error codes emitted by any host IPC handler.
21	 *
22	 * A regression guard in schemas.test.ts asserts this set exactly —
23	 * adding or removing a code is a protocol change and must be done
24	 * with intent.
25	 */
26	export const IpcErrorCodeSchema = z.enum([
27	  'SESSION_INVALID',
28	  'HOST_UNAVAILABLE',
29	  'VALIDATION',
30	  'HOOK_REJECTED',
31	  'NOT_FOUND',
32	  'INTERNAL',
33	]);
34	export type IpcErrorCode = z.infer<typeof IpcErrorCodeSchema>;
35	
36	/** Inner error body — carried inside the envelope below. */
37	export const IpcErrorSchema = z.object({
38	  code: IpcErrorCodeSchema,
39	  message: z.string(),
40	});
41	export type IpcError = z.infer<typeof IpcErrorSchema>;
42	
43	/**
44	 * Wire envelope for an error response. `.strict()` so unknown top-level
45	 * keys are rejected: the envelope shape is frozen (extensions go inside
46	 * `error`, not next to it) and any drift is a protocol bug we want the
47	 * decoder to surface loudly.
48	 */
49	export const IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema = z
50	  .object({
51	    error: IpcErrorSchema,
52	  })
53	  .strict();
54	export type IpcErrorEnvelope = z.infer<typeof IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema>;
55	
56	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	// Typed errors for the sandbox-side IPC client.
58	//
59	// Three shapes, matched to how the runner should react:
60	//
61	//   SessionInvalidError   — the session token the runner holds is no longer
62	//                           honored (401). Terminal. The runner should exit.
63	//                           A retry will not help; the host has told us our
64	//                           session is gone.
65	//
66	//   HostUnavailableError  — the host-side listener is not reachable
67	//                           (ECONNREFUSED, ECONNRESET, EPIPE, missing socket
68	//                           file, per-action timeout). Transient — the
69	//                           client retries with exponential backoff before
70	//                           raising this to the caller.
71	//
72	//   IpcRequestError       — the host accepted the request but rejected it
73	//                           with a structured error envelope (400/404/409/
74	//                           5xx). Carries the wire-level error code and
75	//                           HTTP status. 4xx is a caller bug (don't retry);
76	//                           5xx is transient (the client already retried).
77	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	
79	export class SessionInvalidError extends Error {
80	  constructor(message: string) {
81	    super(message);
82	    this.name = 'SessionInvalidError';
83	  }
84	}
85	
86	export class HostUnavailableError extends Error {
87	  constructor(message: string, cause?: unknown) {
88	    // Use Error's options-bag cause field (ES2022) so stack traces chain
89	    // naturally. Only pass the options object when we actually have a
90	    // cause — exactOptionalPropertyTypes won't let `cause: undefined`
91	    // slip through.
92	    super(message, cause !== undefined ? { cause } : undefined);
93	    this.name = 'HostUnavailableError';
94	  }
95	}
96	
97	export class IpcRequestError extends Error {
98	  constructor(
99	    public readonly code: string, // IpcErrorCode from the wire
100	    public readonly status: number, // HTTP status
101	    message: string,
102	  ) {
103	    super(message);
104	    this.name = 'IpcRequestError';
105	  }
106	}
107

content.text: A dedicated IPC handler exists. Let me read how it maps the `conflict` to a wire code/status:

content.content:
1	import { PluginError } from '@ax/core';
2	import {
3	  ConversationStoreRunnerSessionRequestSchema,
4	  ConversationStoreRunnerSessionResponseSchema,
5	  type ConversationStoreRunnerSessionResponse,
6	} from '@ax/ipc-protocol';
7	import {
8	  internalError,
9	  logInternalError,
10	  mapPluginError,
11	  validationError,
12	} from '../errors.js';
13	import type { ActionHandler } from './types.js';
14	
15	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	// POST /conversation.store-runner-session
17	//
18	// Phase C runner-owned sessions: mints the binding from the runner's native
19	// session_id (the SDK-level resumable handle the runner just observed in its
20	// `system/init` event) to the conversation row that owns the chat. The
21	// host will use this id later to ask the SDK to resume the in-runner
22	// session instead of replaying turns from the host DB.
23	//
24	// Authz pattern (gate lives strictly on the bus impl side):
25	//
26	//   1. The IPC server's auth gate already resolved the runner's bearer
27	//      token to ctx.userId (Week 9.5; ipc-server/listener.ts).
28	//   2. The runner sends `{ conversationId, runnerSessionId }` in the body
29	//      (request schema is .strict — extra fields fail 400 here, before
30	//      the bus is touched, so a malicious runner can't smuggle a foreign
31	//      userId).
32	//   3. We pass `parsed.data` straight through to the service hook.
33	//      conversations:store-runner-session reads `ctx.userId` directly
34	//      inside `storeRunnerSession()` — so we deliberately do NOT add
35	//      userId to the input. Adding it would be noise the bus ignores.
36	//   4. The bus impl runs a userId-scoped UPDATE: a runner whose ctx.userId
37	//      doesn't own `conversationId` will see `not-found`, never the row.
38	//      No `agents:resolve` round-trip — the orchestrator already gated the
39	//      user at agent:invoke entry (Phase B posture).
40	//
41	// PluginError mapping (via mapPluginError):
42	//   - `invalid-payload` → 400 VALIDATION
43	//   - `not-found`       → 404 NOT_FOUND  (cross-tenant or unknown row)
44	//   - `conflict`        → 409 HOOK_REJECTED  (different runnerSessionId
45	//                                            already bound to this row)
46	//   - everything else   → 500 INTERNAL
47	//
48	// On success the bus returns void; we shape the wire response as
49	// `{ ok: true }` and re-parse it through the response schema for
50	// shape-drift defense.
51	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	
53	export const conversationStoreRunnerSessionHandler: ActionHandler = async (
54	  rawPayload,
55	  ctx,
56	  bus,
57	) => {
58	  const parsed = ConversationStoreRunnerSessionRequestSchema.safeParse(rawPayload);
59	  if (!parsed.success) {
60	    return validationError(
61	      `conversation.store-runner-session: ${parsed.error.message}`,
62	    );
63	  }
64	
65	  try {
66	    // The bus impl reads ctx.userId itself; we must NOT smuggle userId
67	    // into input here. Passing parsed.data directly keeps the boundary
68	    // honest — input fields are exactly what the .strict schema let
69	    // through.
70	    await bus.call<
71	      { conversationId: string; runnerSessionId: string },
72	      void
73	    >('conversations:store-runner-session', ctx, parsed.data);
74	  } catch (err) {
75	    logInternalError(ctx.logger, 'conversation.store-runner-session', err);
76	    if (err instanceof PluginError) return mapPluginError(err);
77	    return internalError();
78	  }
79	
80	  // Defense-in-depth: re-parse the literal response shape before
81	  // sending. If a future change to the response schema drifts the wire
82	  // shape, this surfaces as an internal error here rather than as an
83	  // invalid frame downstream.
84	  const checked = ConversationStoreRunnerSessionResponseSchema.safeParse({
85	    ok: true,
86	  });
87	  if (!checked.success) {
88	    logInternalError(
89	      ctx.logger,
90	      'conversation.store-runner-session',
91	      new Error(`response shape drift: ${checked.error.message}`),
92	    );
93	    return internalError();
94	  }
95	  return {
96	    status: 200,
97	    body: checked.data satisfies ConversationStoreRunnerSessionResponse,
98	  };
99	};
100

String:   // killed-before-commit turn leaves it NULL → the retry starts fresh cleanly.

  // self-healing — leave the flag unset so the next accepted commit (or the
  // final commit) retries; the turn already streamed to the user, so failing
  // the run now would be incoherent.
  async function bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded(): Promise<void> {
    const convId = conversationId;
    const sdkSessionId = transcriptSessionId;
    if (
      runnerSessionIdSent ||
      convId === null ||
      runnerSessionId !== null ||
      sdkSessionId === null
    ) {
      return;
    }
    try {
      await client.call('conversation.store-runner-session', {
        conversationId: convId,
        runnerSessionId: sdkSessionId,
      });
      runnerSessionIdSent = true;
    } catch (err) {
      process.stderr.write(
        `runner: conversation.store-runner-session failed (will retry on next commit): ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}\n`,
      );
    }
  }

| 2026-05-24 | F2a Codex finding 2 (High) ACCEPTED: bind failure must distinguish 409 conflict (terminal) from transient (self-heal) | My initial swallow-all "self-healing" was too broad. A `conversation.store-runner-session` 409 (HOOK_REJECTED, per ipc-core/handlers/conversation-store-runner-session.ts) means another runner already bound the conversation to a DIFFERENT id — a concurrent fresh-boot race. Swallowing+retrying lets the LOSER keep streaming + committing an orphan transcript, defeating the host's once-only bind. Fix: `bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded` re-throws on `IpcRequestError && status===409` → propagated past both commit catches → outer catch → `terminated` (surfaced by F2b); transient (network/5xx, client already retried 5xx) stays self-healing. The 409 in the commit try can only come from the bind (commitNotifyWithResync swallows its own IPC errors). | Keep swallow-all (rejected: orphan + double-bind race, Codex-flagged); make ALL bind failures fatal (rejected: fatal-after-a-streamed-turn on a transient blip is incoherent UX) |
| 2026-05-24 | F2a Codex finding 3 (Medium) ACCEPTED: self-heal test rewritten to the realistic multi-turn shape | My first self-heal test relied on the post-`result` FINAL commit retrying the bind — but in production the final drain commit is usually empty (null) because the per-turn commit already captured the turn. Rewrote it: turn-1 per-turn bundle + turn-2 per-turn bundle + final → null (`mockResolvedValueOnce` chain), bind transient-fails on turn 1 and succeeds on turn 2's accepted commit. Proves the retry comes from the NEXT TURN, matching prod. | Keep the final-commit-retry test (rejected: optimistic vs prod where final is null) |
| 2026-05-24 | F2a Codex finding 1 (High — demotion-path orphan) DEFERRED to a tracked follow-up, NOT fixed in this PR | When the resume guard demotes a stale binding (bound id with no resumable transcript), the row stays bound to the stale id (the host bind is once-only — a rebind would `conflict`), so `conversations:get` reads the stale path and the fresh transcript is orphaned; this repeats every boot. Deferring is defensible because: (a) the ROOT fix means `runner_session_id` is set only after a durable commit, so NEW conversations can never form a stale binding — the demotion path is reachable ONLY for pre-existing rows already broken by the very bug being fixed (which TODAY crash on resume) or a hypothetical future materialize/git regression; (b) my fix turns those from a hard `exit 1` crash into a functional-but-empty-history chat — a strict improvement; (c) full repair needs a host-side rebind/overwrite capability (new IPC action + hook + boundary review) = a separate scoped change; (d) making the demotion path attempt a rebind WITHOUT host support would hit the once-only conflict → with finding-2's terminal-conflict that becomes a crash-LOOP for legacy rows — worse UX than silent-functional. The gate therefore stays `runnerSessionId===null` (demotion never binds). Tracked in TODO.md (host-rebind repair for legacy/regression stale rows). | Fix in-PR via a host rebind hook (rejected: cross-plugin scope creep on a runner-side fix; gated on whether legacy stale rows matter in practice); make demotion bind+conflict-terminate (rejected: crash-loop for legacy rows) |
EOF
echo "appended findings decisions"

lines: - [x] ~~**F2a — runner exits 1 replaying an interrupted transcript on retry.**~~ — fixed (PR for `worktree-f2a-resume-no-conversation-fix`). A **live SDK repro** (claude-agent-sdk 0.2.119, 2026-05-24) **disproved** the static "truncated / dangling-tool_use" hypothesis: the SDK resume is robust to both a truncated trailing line AND a dangling tool_use. The real crash is `Error: Claude Code returned an error result: No conversation found with session ID: X`, thrown by `query({ resume: X })` whenever the materialized jsonl for X has **no parseable user/assistant message**. Root cause: commits fire only at turn-end, but the host bind (`conversation.store-runner-session`) fired at `system/init` (~1s in, before durability) — a turn killed in that window persisted a `runner_session_id` pointing at nothing → the retry's resume crashed. Fix = (root) defer the bind to the first host-ACCEPTED turn-end commit (`bindRunnerSessionIfNeeded`, fresh-boot only, self-healing) so `runner_session_id` is set IFF a resumable transcript is durable → killed-before-commit leaves it NULL → retry starts fresh; + (defense-in-depth) a `hasResumableTranscript()` resume guard that omits `resume` instead of letting the SDK hard-crash. No host change; once-only bind invariant intact. Covered by unit tests; cluster walk below.

- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (F2a) on kind — image-rebuild loop required.** Runner code is baked into the image ([[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]), so the fast hostPath loop won't cover it. Walk: start a NEW chat → during the FIRST turn kill the serving pod (`kubectl -n ax-next-runners delete pod <pod>`) → click retry → assert the retry **completes** (not stuck on "Starting sandbox…", not a crash-loop). Confirm via `kubectl -n ax-next-runners logs <retry-pod>` that there is NO `No conversation found with session ID` and the conversation row's `runner_session_id` is bound only after the turn commits. Auth via [[reference_headless_authed_chat_kind]]. Covered by unit tests (turn-end-uuid + main); the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.

+- [ ] **F2a follow-up: host-side rebind to repair a stale `runner_session_id` (legacy/regression rows).** The F2a resume guard demotes a bound-but-non-resumable session to a fresh start, but cannot rebind the conversation row to the new SDK session id — the host bind is once-only (`conflict` on a different value), so the row stays pointed at the stale id and `conversations:get` shows empty history (repeats every boot). This is reachable ONLY for rows already broken by the pre-fix init-time bind (which used to *crash* — now functional-but-empty) or a future materialize/git regression. Full repair needs a host capability to overwrite `runner_session_id` (e.g. a `conversations:rebind-runner-session` hook + IPC action — safe because the runner only demotes after proving the bound transcript is absent in the materialized workspace), called from the demotion path at the first accepted commit. Pick up if legacy stale rows prove to matter in practice.

- [ ] **Pre-existing (noted during F2a, out of scope): turn-end `turnId` uses the boot `runnerSessionId`, not `transcriptSessionId`** (`main.ts` turn-end emissions). On a fresh first turn `runnerSessionId` is null, so no `turnId` is emitted for that turn's `event.turn-end` (drop-turn / routines silence-token can't refer back to it). Switch those reads to `transcriptSessionId` if a real caller needs first-turn turnIds.

- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (F1 + F2b) on kind.** Host-side TS → fast hostPath dist loop (no image rebuild). F1: any turn-error → red row → start a new turn → assert the row resets to "Thinking…" then hides (no stale stuck row; reload not required). F2b: kill the serving pod mid-turn → click retry → assert the retry either completes (only once F2a is fixed) OR surfaces error+retry again — **never** stuck on "Starting sandbox…". Auth via [[reference_headless_authed_chat_kind]]. Covered by unit tests (orchestrator + sse + agent-status); the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.

- [ ] **Faults B/D (host bounce / network drop → silent finalize) remain a separate pre-existing finding.** Out of scope for F1/F2b (different root cause — the client synthesizes a `done`-less close and surfaces nothing; a transport `flush()` concern, not an orchestrator-terminated-outcome concern). Tracked by chat-qa-sweep ([[project_chat_qa_sweep_2026_05_23]]).

git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(F2a): treat a 409 bind conflict as terminal; realistic self-heal test

Addresses Codex review of the F2a fix:
- The deferred bind now distinguishes a 409 conflict (HOOK_REJECTED — the
  conversation is already bound to a DIFFERENT runner session, a concurrent
  fresh-boot race) from a transient failure. A 409 is re-thrown past both
  commit catches so the losing runner TERMINATES instead of silently streaming
  and committing an orphan transcript (the host's once-only bind relies on the
  loser stopping). Transient failures (network/5xx) stay self-healing.
- The self-heal regression test now models production: turn-1 + turn-2 per-turn
  bundles with a null final drain commit, so the bind retry comes from the next
  turn's accepted commit (not the optimistic final-commit path).
- New test: a 409 bind conflict → run exits 1 with a `terminated` chat-end.

TODO: a host-side rebind capability to repair a stale runner_session_id for
legacy/regression rows (the demotion path can't rebind through the once-only
host bind) — documented as a follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git log --oneline -2
